metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_4065
metadata.comment:
Rationale: The paper uses high-resolution membrane capacitance (Cm) measurement combined with Ca2+-uncaging stimuli to monitor the effect of Unc31 KO in DCV exocytosis (as described in PMID:18031683).

Figure 1A: Under Step-like elevations in intracellular calcium, membrane capacitance was significantly reduced in Unc31 mutant (unc-31e928) relative to WT indicating that Unc31 mutants defect exocytosis in ALA neurons of C. elegans. The exocytotic defect was fully rescued by reintroduction of full-length unc-31 cDNA.. Experimental description: The 3780-bp unc-31 cDNA (derived from PMID: 16272411) under the control of the rab-3 promoter was constructed for genetic rescue. The 1.2 kb rab-3 promoter was amplified by PCR and subcloned to pPD49.26 (1997 Fire Lab Vector Kit) to gain pPD49.26-Prab-3.
